paper_title: Materials and Methods of Schlumbergera Cultivation and Pollination Studies

methods.description: This method describes the propagation and growth conditions for Schlumbergera cultivars used in the study, including temperature settings and shading to control photosynthetic photon flux.

steps.description: All cultivars were propagated vegetatively from stock plants maintained at the Univ. of Massachusetts, Amherst.
steps.searchable_summary: propagate Schlumbergera cultivars vegetatively

steps.description: Plants were grown in glasshouses with temperature setpoints of 18/22C (heat/vent).
steps.searchable_summary: set glasshouse temperature to 18/22C

steps.description: Shading compound was applied to the glass to maintain photosynthetic photon flux (PPF) below ≈650 µmol·m -2 ·s -1.
steps.searchable_summary: apply shading compound to control PPF

methods.description: This method outlines the pollination process for Schlumbergera flowers, including the use of compatible pollen and the timing of pollination.

steps.description: Pollination was performed by rubbing recently dehisced anthers on the stigmatic lobes.
steps.searchable_summary: perform pollination by rubbing anthers on stigmas

steps.description: Each flower was pollinated once using pollen collected from two or more flowers.
steps.searchable_summary: use pollen from multiple flowers for pollination

methods.description: This method investigates the effect of harvest timing on seed maturity and germination rates in Schlumbergera.

steps.description: Seventy-five flowers of '88-50' were pollinated on the day of anthesis with compatible ('Dark Marie') pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: pollinate '88-50' flowers with 'Dark Marie' pollen

steps.description: Fruit were harvested at 4,5,6,7, and 8 months after pollination.
steps.searchable_summary: harvest fruit at specified intervals post-pollination

steps.description: Seeds that were shriveled or collapsed were discarded, and the remaining seeds were counted and germinated in covered petri dishes.
steps.searchable_summary: discard non-viable seeds and germinate viable seeds

steps.description: Percent seed germination was calculated for each treatment and the data was arcsin-transformed before analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and transform seed germination percentage

steps.description: All variables were analyzed by SAS Institute's (1985) General Linear Model (GLM) procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze data using GLM procedure

methods.method_name: Stigmatic Surface and Pollen Grain Morphology
methods.description: This method examines the morphology of the stigmatic surface and mature pollen grains using microscopy techniques.

steps.description: Five styles of 'Dark Marie' were collected at anthesis, fixed in 2% (w/v) glutaraldehyde in 0.1 M phosphate buffer (pH 7), dehydrated through an ethanol series, critical-point dried in CO2, and coated with 200 to 300 A of gold-palladium.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare 'Dark Marie' styles for electron microscopy

steps.description: Stigmas were viewed on a scanning electron microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: view stigmas using scanning electron microscopy

steps.description: Pollen of 'Buckleyi' and 'Dark Marie' was collected, fixed in 3 ethanol: 1 glacial acetic acid (v/v) for >24 h, and stained with mithramycin A.
steps.searchable_summary: fix and stain pollen for microscopy

steps.description: Slides were examined with a Zeiss epifluorescence microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: examine stained pollen using epifluorescence microscopy

methods.description: This method investigates the kinetics of pollen adhesion and germination on the stigmatic surface over time.

steps.description: Whole flowers of 'Eva' and '88-29' were collected on the day of anthesis and placed in covered petri dishes containing blotter paper moistened with deionized water.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and prepare flowers for pollen adhesion study

steps.description: Compatible pollen ('Dark Marie') was applied to the stigmatic surface of each flower with a fine artist's brush.
steps.searchable_summary: apply 'Dark Marie' pollen to stigmas

steps.description: At specified time intervals, stigmas were excised and placed in fixative, then shaken to dislodge non-adherent grains.
steps.searchable_summary: excise and fix stigmas, dislodge non-adherent pollen

steps.description: Stigmas and pollen were examined using visible light microscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: examine stigmas and pollen with light microscopy

methods.method_name: Pollen Tube Growth and Fruit Set Kinetics
methods.description: This method examines the kinetics of pollen tube growth and the effect of stylar excision timing on fruit set.

steps.description: Twelve plants of '88-50' with mature flower buds were transferred to a controlled-environment chamber.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer '88-50' plants to controlled environment

steps.description: Flowers were pollinated on the day of anthesis with compatible ('88-68') pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: pollinate flowers with '88-68' pollen

steps.description: Styles were excised at specified time intervals after pollination and fixed for examination.
steps.searchable_summary: excise and fix styles at intervals post-pollination

steps.description: Examine styles with an epifluorescence microscope to measure pollen tube growth.
steps.searchable_summary: measure pollen tube growth with epifluorescence microscopy

steps.description: Calculate percent fruit set and analyze data using chi-square analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate fruit set percentage and perform chi-square analysis
